candidates.stmt:
I grew up in Hoyland Common, went to Kirk Balk School and have worked in the ward. I have seen the devastating impact the cuts have had on our community.

If you place your trust in me on 14 December, I will work to help families, reduce social isolation and give older people the support they deserve. As a Mum, I want to make sure we improve the opportunities for our young people in Barnsley.

I’m a family man, raised on a farm, who’s worked since I was 17 and served 11 years in the military.
I believe in hard work, responsibility, and getting the job done.
I’m standing as a reform candidate to deliver for the local community in Barnsley.
I’ll make sure our voice is heard loud and clear on the issues that matter.
I’m not a career politician—just someone ready to stand up and deliver real change.

I was born and raised in Barnsley ,my early life was in Wombwell emersed within the mining community and all that brought with it over the years .I grew up eith a strong sense of community and my father drummed into me that I must join a Union which I have adhered toall my working life 
Slowly I became aware how our community in Barnsley is being eroded and I asked myself why and questioned , this wouldn't happen in the past .
It needs sorting ,we are loseing our history , our very way of life is vanishing .I want the old Barnsley back ..do you ?? That is why Im standing for Reform

Since first being elected in 2021 I have worked hard to be a strong voice for Darfield and surrounding communities in the Town Hall, to gain improvements to our area and increase the quality of life for all. My one aim has always been to do the very best I can for the people of Darfield. It has been a challenge but it's one I relish. This role has never been so as important whilst local families struggle under the Tory cost of living crisis.  the experience I have gained will enable me to fight for Darfield's corner during these difficult times.   I am not afraid to take on the touch challenges we face. I am not afraid to take on companies and agencies who serve our community but could do better such as Yorkshire Water, BT Openreach, E-On and the Environment Agency.

I'm not afraid to stand up and fight for what's best for our community whether that's housing, policing, community cohesion or planning.

I'm proud to represent you in Barnsley Council; with your support that work can continue.

Kevin Osborne March 2024

If you're a Darfield resident and have any questions or issues you want to discuss, get in touch: ✉️ Kevin4Darfield@mail.com ☎ 07507 864863 📲 @Kevin4Darfield (Facebook) 📲 Kevin4Darfield (Instagram)

As a local resident, it is a real privilege to have been selected as Labour’s candidate for Darton East Ward in the local elections on Thursday 7th May 2026.

I’ve lived in Darton for over 28 years and spent 40 years working in the NHS as a Registered Nurse and Health Visitor. I care deeply about our community and believe local representatives should be visible, approachable, and active all year round not just at election time.

I am already working across Darton East, listening to residents, taking action, and helping to get things sorted. I regularly report issues such as potholes, fly-tipping and environmental concerns, and work closely with Barnsley Council, local police, and community partners to follow issues through to action.

If elected, my priorities will include:

* Being available and holding regular surgeries to support residents and local businesses
* Improving roads, transport links, local services and community infrastructure
* Continuing to campaign for better, more reliable bus services across South Yorkshire
* Supporting young people to access local youth services and have a stronger voice in decision-making
* Working with partners to help keep our communities safe and reduce anti-social behaviour

I am an active member of Darton East Ward Alliance and volunteer regularly at Mapplewell and Staincross Village Hall. I also support local environmental groups including Voice for Darton and the Greenspace Group, and take part in regular litter picks alongside Barnsley Council teams.

In 2022, I was honoured to receive a Volunteer of the Year award from Darton East Ward Alliance, recognising my commitment to the local area.

I am proud to stand for Darton East and would be grateful for your support.

🗳️ On Thursday 7th May 2026, please use one of your votes for Teresa Wilcockson.

If you need help with a local issue or would like to get in touch, please don’t hesitate to contact me.

Hello, my name's Luca, your Reform UK candidate.

I'm a young lad who wants to see change, improvement and a better way of living for everyone. I'm deeply passionate about serving the community and standing up for what I believe in... YOU! I believe in putting YOU at the heart of decisions that affect your everyday life. As someone who values transparency and accountability, I believe in putting power back into your hands so you can have a say in the decisions that impact your life. 

I'm here to listen, understand and advocate for YOUR needs. I'll always be available to YOU, the people that I hope to represent. Together, let's build a brighter future for our community.

Thank you.

I’ve spent my whole working life in South Yorkshire – and mainly in mining villages in the Dearne Valley. So I know Barnsley South’s communities really well. My early career was to reduce pollution of the River Dearne – which now supports fish along its length.  

I moved with my wife to the borough 26 years ago, and I worked for Barnsley Council in various environmental roles for 19 years. I have managed the maintenance of the Trans-Pennine Trail and Council nature reserves, plus I have been the advisor on wildlife issues.  I want a Review of the Council’s ‘Local Plan’ as I think too much land has been taken out of Green Belt for development. This is one of the reasons I got active with the Green Party – that and the policy of Universal Basic Income, which I think tries to make the UK a fairer country for people. I’m Barnsley Green Party’s Communications Officer. As well as being a passionate naturalist, I’m a keen racing cyclist – being the Chairman of Birdwell Wheelers. I also campaign for safer roads.

Being born in Yorkshire; I believe in reclaiming our Yorkshire pride, challenging the status quo of the labour old guard and standing up for our vulnerable.

Its time to take control and look after our own.

Why Should You Vote?

If you are undecided about who to vote for in this general election or have decided not to vote or you think voting for someone that does not represent one of the main parties is a wasted vote, then think again.  It is time to send a clear message to those that take your vote for granted that they must earn your vote.  If you do not vote then they will assume that you are happy with the way things are. We have just seen the VE day commemorative events to remember the boys and girls that made so many sacrifices for this country, including sacrificing their lives and are we going to thank them for fighting for this country by just giving up now? We need to make a stand to protect our rights, our freedom of speech, our culture and our country. 

This country is slowly sinking or some might say stinking because of years of mismanagement of our resources and our weak laws, always at the hands of the elected Labour and Conservative representatives.  The only thing they represent is their own interests. It is time to elect real people that have lived in the real world, that have the heart and passion to fight to the death to save our country. I would like you to vote for me but even if you decide to vote for someone else, do not give the two main parties more time to destroy you and our country.

My Background Has Given Me Strong Views on Particular Issues

I was born in a modest house in London, that my family worked hard to pay for. The law of compulsory purchase was introduced by the Labour government at that time and our home was seized and our family evicted, that was my first experience of homelessness: I was just under five years old.  Even now I can remember us being dragged out of the house by police and being on the street for hours in the cold and walking around in the night trying to find somewhere to sleep.  When I was older and was homeless again it was easier to cope with but no human or even an animal should ever experience homelessnes

I’m standing for election because I believe local politics works best when it puts community first.

I’m a local lad, and I want to give back to the community that has given so much to me. I’ve spent the last few years representing young people across Barnsley, listening to their concerns and helping make sure their voices are taken seriously. That experience has shaped how I see democracy by ensuring decisions made with residents, not done to them.

Although I’m young, I care deeply about this area and the people who live here. I bring energy, commitment, and a genuine desire to listen and work alongside others to make a real difference.

If elected, my priorities are clear:

- Putting community first in every decision,

- Supporting our brilliant community groups and volunteers

- Keeping pride in our towns and villages, with clean, safe streets and well-looked-after public spaces

- Promoting co-operative values, so people have a real say in the things that affect their everyday lives

I believe politics should be about fairness, participation and trust. It shouldn’t just happen at election time, and it should always be rooted in the everyday lives of local people.

I’m standing as a Labour candidate because those values reflect how I want to serve you, by working together, strengthening communities, and making sure no one is left behind.

I hope to earn your support and have the chance to represent our area with honesty, hard work and pride.

I will be back and standing in the Monk Bretton Ward in May 2020 after been successful in being selected 

Thank You

Local campaigner Peter Fielding is standing for election to Barnsley Council to fight to protect our precious countryside and green spaces.
Peter is fighting Barnsley Labour's plans to build 5000 houses on greenbelt land including a huge area between Higham and Gawber.
Peter is also a major Penny Pie Park campaigner opposing Barnsley Councils plans to replace it with a 3 lane roundabout.

Peter says "As well as ripping the green heart out of the community this roundabout will create more air quality issues right next to the biggest school in Europe".

Please support Peter in these local elections to Barnsley Town Hall.

Phil Lofts served in the Army. He is a retired Community Education Officer and Project Manager. He is an experienced councillor who has served Old Town for 10 years and whose family has been in Old Town for several generations. Phil is a member of Friends of the Earth, Greenpeace, RSPB, and Barnsley Civic Trust.

Jo Newing worked as a Nurse at Barnsley Hospital. She has since worked with homeless and vulnerable people, and most recently with Barnsley CAMHs, supporting neurodivergent young people. She is an experienced councillor who has served Old Town for eight years. Jo is also a member of the Humanist Society and Greenpeace.

Brandon Green is a qualified Secondary School Teacher and Historian who has a keen interest in the history and culture of Old Town. He previously served as a Barnsley Youth Councillor for four years and campaigned to improve access to public transport and to create better work opportunities for young people.

I am a devoted husband and father of two amazing little girls. They are my number one reason for standing as the Reform UK candidate for Penistone & Stocksbridge.

Living in our amazingly beautiful constituency sometimes you can forget just how badly our country has been run for the past 20+ years. We've a southern border that is invaded daily by 100's of fighting age men. An immigration system happily letting 25,000 people a week come in. The vast majority being unskilled and uneducated so will only be able to live here by taking money from you, the taxpayer. We’ve got the highest taxes since the end of WWII. We have an NHS failing on almost every measurable scale. Your chances of getting a GP or dentist appointment are slim to none. Kids rammed in to overcrowded classrooms whilst they are being force fed sex education from as young as 8 and a Government that thinks they know what is best for your children. Farmers being crushed by supermarket bullying, mass bureaucracy and red tape. Whilst we import poor quality substitutes from abroad.

So, to tell you what I would do for you if you lend me your vote:

I will fight for your family, just like I am for mine. I will fight to stop the Government stealing the money you earn. I will fight to stop the mass importation of the third world and to stop us, the tax payer, paying for them to live here. I will fight for our kids to be able to go to school and have fun, be kids. Not be indoctrinated with the toxic ideologies that all the other main parties are pushing. I will fight for us, for our country, for our British values and most importantly for the great people in the Penistone & Stocksbridge Constituency.

 Edward Dillingham Reform UK Let's make Britain Great

I’m proud to be your local Labour candidate for the Wombwell Ward.

Wombwell is my home. I’ll always fight for our community’s fair share.

My pledges to you:

* Fair recovery from Covid * Investment in Wombwell High Street * Cleaner, Greener Wombwell * Stronger, Safer Communities * Approachable and accountable

Vote Barnsley Labour on Thursday 6th May.
